free_text: Distribution of Partial Radiation Widths in
238U(n,gamma)239U
Oak Ridge electron accelerator
Pulsed time-of-flight technique.
GeLi. detector 10 cm3. GeLi shielded from
scattered neutrons by a 1.5 in. thickness of Li2O3
and paraffin and 0.125 in. Pb. Gamma-ray resolution
of 4.4 keV FWHM at 4.0 MeV.
0.25 in. thick x 2.5 in. diam. of depleted uranium
Gamma-ray energy calibration made relative to
impurity gamma-ray peaks from neutron capture in
hydrogen and iron.
Intensity normalization was done for the 6.7 eV
resonance at the 22 m flight path of the fast-
chopper facility at the high-flux beam reactor
at Brookhaven. The absolute intensity of the 6.7 eV
uranium resonance was measured relative to the
4.9 eV resonance in Au and 11.9 eV resonance in Pt.
Runs corrected for gain shift before being
combined together. Total spectrum corrected for
background.
DR
The errors contains the contribution
from the errors in the net peak area as well
as an estimate of the error in the background
correction to the total spectral counts.
Data taken from table 4 of the journal article
